title: DecisionBrief
description: Phase 0 output: the framed decision and the panel convened to decide it.

question.description: The crisp decision to be made.

options.description: Concrete OPENING options that seed the slate. Do not invent — derive from the question and context. The panel can add, reframe, split, or merge options as it deliberates; this is a starting menu, not a closed ballot.

summary.description: One line on what choosing this means.

constraints.description: Hard constraints the decision must satisfy.

successCriteria.description: What a good outcome looks like.

contextPack.description: Condensed, citable shared context (repo facts, prior ADRs, MCP findings) given to every member.

panel.description: Real people in real roles this decision would pull into a room. NOT debate-archetypes (no 'pragmatist'/'devils-advocate'). No names here: the script assigns neutral names decoupled from role.

role.description: Realistic role/title, e.g. 'platform reliability engineer', 'person who owns the cost line'.

focus.description: Example things someone in this role tends to weigh. Orientation, not orders to argue a side.

disposition.description: One line placing them at a believable point on a spectrum (risk appetite, change vs. stability...). Distinct and human; never a caricature, never 'extreme'.

model.description: Default to sonnet; reserve opus for the facilitator and at most one pivotal role; haiku for lighter voices. An all-opus panel both wastes the flagship and collapses prior diversity.

facilitator.description: Exactly one member is true: they run the room and write up the decision (and still hold real views).

chairModel.description: Model for the facilitator's compile/convergence/synthesis calls. Equals the facilitator's own model.
